rules.message: Uses tasks/result from the experimental 2025-11-25 Tasks API. The graduated Tasks feature has no tasks/result method.
rules.replacement: Migrate to the graduated Tasks lifecycle: tasks/get, tasks/update, tasks/cancel, with task handles returned by tools/call.

rules.message: Uses tasks/list from the experimental 2025-11-25 Tasks API. tasks/list is removed entirely in the 2026-07-28 revision because it cannot be scoped safely without sessions.
rules.replacement: Track task IDs on the requestor side and poll them individually with tasks/get.

rules.message: Uses the notifications/tasks/status notification from the experimental 2025-11-25 Tasks API.
rules.replacement: Migrate to the graduated Tasks lifecycle: poll with tasks/get and receive updates via tasks/update.

rules.message: References the CreateTaskResult type from the experimental 2025-11-25 Tasks API.
rules.replacement: Migrate to the graduated Tasks feature, where tools/call returns task handles directly.

rules.message: Uses the io.modelcontextprotocol/related-task _meta key from the experimental 2025-11-25 Tasks API.
rules.replacement: Migrate to the graduated Tasks lifecycle (tasks/get, tasks/update, tasks/cancel).

rules.message: Uses the provisional io.modelcontextprotocol/model-immediate-response _meta key from the experimental 2025-11-25 Tasks API.
rules.replacement: Migrate to the graduated Tasks lifecycle, which formalizes immediate results on tools/call.

rules.message: References the execution.taskSupport tool declaration from the experimental 2025-11-25 Tasks API.
rules.replacement: Re-declare task capability using the graduated Tasks extension and verify the field against the 2026-07-28 schema.

rules.message: Uses the Roots feature (roots/list or notifications/roots/list_changed), deprecated in the 2026-07-28 revision with a 12-month window.
rules.replacement: Pass paths or scope explicitly via tool parameters, resource URIs, or server configuration.

rules.message: Calls list_roots(), part of the deprecated Roots feature (12-month window).
rules.replacement: Pass paths or scope explicitly via tool parameters or server configuration.

rules.message: Calls listRoots(), part of the deprecated Roots feature (12-month window).
rules.replacement: Pass paths or scope explicitly via tool parameters or server configuration.

rules.message: Uses the Sampling feature (sampling/createMessage), deprecated in the 2026-07-28 revision with a 12-month window.
rules.replacement: Call your LLM provider's API directly from the server instead of sampling through the client.

rules.message: Calls create_message(). If this is MCP sampling through the client session, it is deprecated (12-month window).
rules.replacement: Call your LLM provider's API directly from the server.

rules.message: Calls createMessage(). If this is MCP sampling through the client, it is deprecated (12-month window).
rules.replacement: Call your LLM provider's API directly from the server.

rules.message: Uses protocol-level Logging (logging/setLevel), deprecated in the 2026-07-28 revision with a 12-month window.
rules.replacement: Log to stderr for stdio transports, or emit structured telemetry via OpenTelemetry.

rules.message: Uses the protocol-level log notification (notifications/message), deprecated in the 2026-07-28 revision with a 12-month window.
rules.replacement: Log to stderr for stdio transports, or emit structured telemetry via OpenTelemetry.

rules.message: Calls send_log_message(), part of deprecated protocol-level Logging (12-month window).
rules.replacement: Log to stderr for stdio transports, or emit structured telemetry via OpenTelemetry.

rules.message: Calls sendLoggingMessage(), part of deprecated protocol-level Logging (12-month window).
rules.replacement: Log to stderr for stdio transports, or emit structured telemetry via OpenTelemetry.

rules.message: References the Mcp-Session-Id header. Protocol-level sessions are removed in the 2026-07-28 revision; any request can land on any server instance.
rules.replacement: Remove session handling; carry state in explicit handles passed as tool arguments.

rules.message: Configures sessionIdGenerator on the Streamable HTTP transport. Protocol-level sessions are removed in the 2026-07-28 revision.
rules.replacement: Run the transport stateless; carry state in explicit handles passed as tool arguments.

rules.message: Hooks the initialize handshake. The 2026-07-28 revision removes the handshake; protocol version, client info, and capabilities travel in _meta on every request.
rules.replacement: Read client info and capabilities from request _meta instead of an initialize hook.

rules.message: Uses initialize handshake options. The 2026-07-28 revision removes the handshake; protocol version, client info, and capabilities travel in _meta on every request.
rules.replacement: Read client info and capabilities from request _meta instead of initialization options.

rules.message: Sticky-session routing detected. The 2026-07-28 revision makes sticky routing unnecessary: any MCP request can land on any server instance.
rules.replacement: Drop session affinity from the deployment once the server is stateless.

rules.message: Session store detected. If this holds MCP protocol session state, it is unnecessary under the 2026-07-28 revision.
rules.replacement: Carry state in explicit handles passed as tool arguments; application-level stores keyed by those handles are fine.

rules.message: API-key header auth detected. The 2026-07-28 revision mandates OAuth 2.1 alignment for HTTP transports, including issuer validation per RFC 9207.
rules.replacement: Adopt the OAuth 2.1 authorization flow described in the 2026-07-28 spec.

rules.message: Imports from mcp.server.fastmcp. Python SDK v2 renames FastMCP to MCPServer and drops the old import path entirely.
rules.replacement: Use: from mcp.server import MCPServer. The decorator API carries over.

rules.message: CommonJS require() of the TypeScript SDK. SDK v2 is ESM-only and runs on Node.js 20+, Bun, and Deno.
rules.replacement: Convert the module to ESM and import from the focused v2 packages (@modelcontextprotocol/server or @modelcontextprotocol/client).

rules.message: Imports the monolithic @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package. SDK v2 retires it in favor of focused packages.
rules.replacement: Import from @modelcontextprotocol/server (servers) or @modelcontextprotocol/client (clients), plus the transport adapter you need.

rules.message: package.json depends on the monolithic @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package, retired in SDK v2.
rules.replacement: Depend on @modelcontextprotocol/server or @modelcontextprotocol/client instead.

rules.message: Client calls listTools(). In the 2026-07-28 revision, tools/list results carry ttlMs and cacheScope; clients can cache instead of re-fetching.
rules.replacement: Cache the tools/list result and honor its ttlMs and cacheScope fields.

rules.message: Client calls list_tools(). In the 2026-07-28 revision, tools/list results carry ttlMs and cacheScope; clients can cache instead of re-fetching.
rules.replacement: Cache the tools/list result and honor its ttlMs and cacheScope fields.
